events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need decide pass/fail. Scenario: caliper guide pins dry/seized. Brakes rough-complete. This is non-compliant/defective. Decision fail. Risk high/critical? Dry/seized guide pins can cause uneven braking, reduced braking, safety critical. In automotive assembly quality gate, likely high or critical. FMVSS-adjacent shop practice and OEM specs require guide pins lubricated and free movement. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 70. Confidence 0.95. Findings: guide pins lack lubrication and are seized, caliper cannot float/freely move, brake pad wear and braking performance compromised. Actions: remove/rework caliper, clean/lubricate guide pins with approved brake grease, verify free movement, reassemble and inspect, hold at quality gate. Rationale. References: OEM brake assembly specs, FMVSS 135 (hydraulic brake systems) performance, shop standard work. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Caliper guide pins are dry and seized, preventing proper caliper float.",
    "Seized guide pins will cause uneven pad wear and reduced or asymmetric braking performance.",
    "Condition violates OEM assembly specifications and FMVSS-adjacent brake performance requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Hold the brake assembly at the quality gate and reject the unit.",
    "Remove or disassemble the affected caliper and clean the guide pins and bores.",
    "Apply manufacturer-approved high-temperature brake caliper grease to the guide pins.",
    "Verify pins move freely and smoothly after lubrication.",
    "Reassemble, torque to OEM specification, and re-inspect before release."
  ],
  "rationale": "Dry/seized caliper guide pins are an installed-defective, non-compliant condition. They prevent the caliper from sliding properly, leading to uneven braking, accelerated wear, and a potential safety-related brake performance failure. The work must fail the quality gate until corrected.",
  "references": [
    "OEM brake caliper assembly and lubrication specifications",
    "FMVSS 135 / brake system performance and assembly practice"
  ]
}
